records.ety: [F. commenter, L. commentari to meditate upon, explain, v. intens. of comminisci, commentus, to reflect upon, invent; com- + the root of meminisse to remember, mens mind. See Mind.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Commented; p. pr. & vb. n. Commenting.]

defs: To make remarks, observations, or criticism; especially, to write notes on the works of an author, with a view to illustrate his meaning, or to explain particular passages; to write annotations; -- often followed by on or upon.
quotes: A physician to comment on your malady.
Critics . . . proceed to comment on him.

defs: A remark, observation, or criticism; gossip; discourse; talk.
quotes: Their lavish comment when her name was named.

defs: A note or observation intended to explain, illustrate, or criticise the meaning of a writing, book, etc.; explanation; annotation; exposition.
quotes: All the volumes of philosophy, With all their comments.
